{"step_by_step_thinking": "The patient is on an implantable cardioverter defibrillator (ICD) and has developed shock. To determine the position and integrity of the ICD, we need a method that can visualize the device and its leads accurately. Computed tomography (CT) with contrast (CECT) can provide detailed images of the chest and help in assessing the position and integrity of the ICD leads. However, MRI is contraindicated in patients with metallic implants like ICDs due to the risk of heating and movement of the metal components. Ultrasound (USG) can show the heart and some parts of the chest but may not provide detailed visualization of the ICD leads. Plain radiographs can show the general location of the ICD and its leads but lack the detail needed to fully assess the integrity of the device. Therefore, the best method among the given options is CECT.", "answer_choice": "A"}
